meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to master SaaS pricing models to overcome a lack of portfolio. An Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship fast.
content.answer_block: Solve the 'no portfolio' trap by building a high-value SaaS Pricing calculator for a niche industry. By demonstrating technical mastery through a functional, interactive pricing tool, you provide immediate value to prospective clients, proving your architecture skills even without a long history of previous client projects.
content.intro:
The blank page of a portfolio is the silent killer of freelance careers. You have the technical chops, but clients see only a void where your track record should be. They don't want to see your code; they want to see how you solve their business problems.

We are going to bridge that gap by building a sophisticated SaaS Pricing Engine. By leveraging AI-augmented workflows, we’ll move from concept to deployment in hours, not weeks. This isn't just a project; it's a demonstration of your ability to translate complex business logic into scalable, production-ready software.

steps.title: Define the Business Logic
steps.description: Establish the variables for your pricing engine, such as MAU, storage limits, and feature gating. This ensures your code isn't just a UI, but a functional business tool.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a schema for a SaaS pricing engine that calculates tiered monthly costs based on user-defined inputs for active users, storage usage, and premium feature toggles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a data model for a tiered pricing engine in TypeScript'

steps.title: Architect the UI Components
steps.description: Build a responsive interface that allows users to toggle pricing plans dynamically. Focus on clean, modular component design.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component using Tailwind CSS that features three pricing cards with hover states, clear CTA buttons, and a monthly/yearly toggle switch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement the pricing component with responsive Tailwind grid layout'

steps.title: Integrate Pricing Logic
steps.description: Write the core calculation function that updates prices in real-time. This proves your ability to handle business logic on the frontend.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust JavaScript function that takes state inputs from the pricing toggle and returns calculated monthly costs for Basic, Pro, and Enterprise tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add the calculation logic to the pricing state manager'

steps.title: Add Professional Polish
steps.description: Implement loading states and input validation to show you care about the user experience, a hallmark of senior-level development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add input validation and loading skeletons to the pricing calculator to ensure a seamless experience during state transitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add framer-motion animations to the pricing card transitions'

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Deploy your project and create a README that explains the 'why' behind your architecture, turning the code into a portfolio-ready case study.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md for this SaaS Pricing Engine project that highlights the tech stack, the business logic, and the scalability of the solution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a high-impact project summary for my portfolio'

pro_tips: Always include a 'Cost per User' breakdown to show you understand unit economics, not just code.
Use mock API responses in your code to simulate real-world data fetching, which impresses technical leads.
Add a 'Download as PDF' feature to your pricing tool; it's a simple utility that adds massive perceived value.
Structure your git commits to show a clear progression from 'Foundation' to 'Optimization' to 'Polish'.
Include a 'Why this stack' section in your README to demonstrate your decision-making maturity as an architect.
content.next_steps: Link this project in your LinkedIn featured section and reach out to three prospective clients in the niche you just built for, using the tool as a conversation starter.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-grade asset that proves your value beyond a resume. Stop waiting for the perfect project and start creating your own authority. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
